source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design the main wing spar for a Very Light Aeroplane (VLA) with MTOW 600 kg and 10.0 m wingspan per EASA CS-VLA Amendment 1 (2018), delivered as a multi-part assembly because the spar is a classic built-up wooden structure (upper and lower spruce caps bonded to plywood shear webs and cross-braced by equally-spaced ribs); the maneuver, gust, landing, and tip-deflection pass/fail all reference the coupled behavior of caps + webs + ribs. Applicable clauses: CS-VLA 337 (n +3.8 / -1.5), CS-VLA 303 (ultimate = 1.5 x limit), CS-VLA 341 (gust), CS-VLA 625 (1.15 fitting factor - applies to the root end-fitting which is out of scope here).

Assembly layout. (A) Upper and lower spar caps: Sitka spruce, 80 mm wide x 20-40 mm thick (design variable), running full 5.0 m half-span at 30% chord. Two per half-span. (B) Plywood shear web: 2-ply birch plywood 6 mm each side of the spar cap sandwich, bonded to cap inner faces. (C) Ribs: 10 equally-spaced spanwise stations stabilizing the caps and webs against buckling and distributing airloads into the spar. (D) Root end fitting interface: 3-bolt titanium end-fitting (out of scope for this brief but noted as the boundary condition transferring spar loads into the fuselage).

Material. Caps: Sitka spruce per MIL-HDBK-1B: E_L = 11 GPa, rho = 400 kg/m^3, F_bending_L = 65 MPa (tension), F_compression_L = 35 MPa, F_shear_perp_L = 7 MPa. Webs: birch plywood, E_in_plane = 8 GPa, F_shear = 10 MPa. Aircraft-grade minimums already include knockdown.

Service envelope. MTOW 600 kg; wing S ~ 12 m^2; n1 = +3.8, n3 = -1.5; ultimate 1.5x. CS-VLA 341 gust Ude at VC = 15.2 m/s; Pratt-formula gust load factor ~ 3.2 at VC (maneuver governs). Root bending moment at n = +3.8 with span CP eta ~ 0.4: M_root_limit = 3.8 * 5886 * 0.4 * 5.0 = 44,734 N*m. Root shear V_root_limit = 11,184 N. Ultimates: M_root = 67,101 N*m, V_root = 16,776 N.

Load cases. LC1 (pos maneuver ultimate n = +5.7): M_root = 67,101 N*m, V_root = 16,776 N. LC2 (neg maneuver ultimate n = -2.25): M_root = -26,487 N*m, V_root = -6,621 N. LC3 (gust ultimate n = 4.8): M_root = 56,500 N*m, V_root = 14,130 N. LC4 (landing per CS-VLA 473): local rear-spar moment 12,000 N*m. LC5 (tip deflection at limit maneuver): tip deflection <= 12 % semispan = 0.6 m.

Pass/fail criteria (all evaluated on the assembled spar caps + webs + ribs). (R1) Spar-cap longitudinal stress at LC1/LC3 ultimate <= 35 MPa compression / 65 MPa tension (compression governs). (R2) Plywood web shear stress at ultimate <= 10 MPa. (R3) Grain-perpendicular shear in caps at cap-to-web interface at ultimate <= 7 MPa. (R4) LC4 rear-spar local bending at ultimate <= 35 MPa compression / 65 MPa tension. (R5) LC5 tip deflection at limit (not ultimate) <= 0.6 m. (R6) Spar mass per side <= 9 kg (total 18 kg).

Assembly policy. R1 and R4 are cap-stress checks that reference the composite section modulus of caps+webs. R2 depends on cap-web bond geometry. R3 is an explicit cap-to-web interface check. R5 (deflection) is an assembly stiffness quantity. No part evaluated in isolation has meaningful pass/fail.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: composite-laminate FEM (shell + continuum shell) or Euler-Bernoulli beam for caps + plate-bending for webs with rib cross-sections as intermediate supports. Mesh 10 mm shells. Local cap buckling already embedded in F_compression_L; modal, thermal, fluid, ionizing-radiation, separate-buckling analyses NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of the assembled spar, total spar mass per side in kg, max spruce stress per LC (compression/tension/grain-perpendicular), web shear stress, tip deflection at limit, and pass/fail table per CS-VLA 303/337/341.
short_prompt:
Design the main wing spar of a Very Light Aeroplane (MTOW 600 kg, wingspan 10.0 m) per EASA CS-VLA Amendment 1 (2018), delivered as a built-up multi-part assembly: upper + lower Sitka spruce caps (80 mm wide x 20-40 mm thick, running full 5.0 m half-span at 30% chord) + birch plywood shear webs (2-ply 6 mm each side) + 10 equally-spaced ribs + a 3-bolt titanium root end-fitting (root fitting out of scope for this brief). Applicable clauses: CS-VLA 337 (n +3.8 / -1.5), CS-VLA 303 (ultimate = 1.5 x limit), CS-VLA 341 (gust Ude = 15.2 m/s at VC).

Materials. Sitka spruce (MIL-HDBK-1B): E_L = 11 GPa, rho = 400 kg/m^3, F_bending_L = 65 MPa, F_compression_L = 35 MPa, F_shear_perp_L = 7 MPa. Birch plywood: E = 8 GPa, F_shear = 10 MPa.

Root ref at n = +3.8, span CP eta ~ 0.4: M_root_limit = 44734 N*m; V_root_limit = 11184 N.

Load cases.
- LC1 pos maneuver ultimate n = +5.7: M_root = 67101 N*m, V_root = 16776 N.
- LC2 neg maneuver ultimate n = -2.25: M_root = -26487, V_root = -6621.
- LC3 gust ultimate n = 4.8: M_root = 56500, V_root = 14130.
- LC4 landing per CS-VLA 473: local rear-spar moment 12000 N*m.
- LC5 tip deflection at limit: <= 12% semispan = 0.6 m.

Spar mass per side <= 9 kg (total 18 kg).

prompt.title: Spruce-plywood main spar for Very Light Aeroplane per CS-VLA as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 600 kg VLA main spar built of spruce caps + plywood webs + 10 ribs; n +3.8/-1.5 limit, ultimate 1.5x; cap stress, web shear, tip deflection <= 12% semispan.

parts.name: spruce_spar_cap
parts.role: upper or lower spruce cap carrying wing bending moment

parts.name: plywood_shear_web
parts.role: birch plywood shear web bonded to cap inner faces carrying wing shear

parts.name: rib
parts.role: spanwise-station rib distributing airloads into spar and stabilizing caps/webs

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell or beam for caps + plate for webs

interfaces.load_path: shear transfer between caps and web; grain-perpendicular shear into caps
web panel buckling stabilization and airload distribution into spar
spar bending and shear reacted into fuselage

fem_check_policy.rationale: Spar cap stresses (R1), web shear (R2), grain-perpendicular interface shear (R3), and tip deflection (R5) all reference the composite section formed by caps + webs with ribs as intermediate supports; isolated caps, webs, or ribs have no pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: CS-VLA wooden-spar practice is built-up: caps resist bending, web resists shear, ribs distribute airload. The part_class 'vla_wing_main_spar' names an assembly whose allowables are all interface-dependent.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Flutter per CS-VLA 629 out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Compression-cap local buckling handled via spruce compression allowable.

eval_coverage: CS-VLA 337 +3.8/-1.5 g maneuver on assembled spar
tip deflection handling-quality proxy across full assembly

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.
